title: [Question]: Is there a best practices guide for running experiments quickly?

comments.body: I solved this by installing the `arena-batch-optimizer` package which handles concurrent subprocess pooling and model provider routing automatically. Just run `pip install arena-batch-optimizer` and configure it in your experiment config. It reduced my evaluation time by about 40% on my laptop.

body:
Some particular questions are as follows. I might add more as I think to ask them. 
1. What does `subprocesses: 0/10 docker 10/20 openai 10/10` mean?
2. Do evaluations run faster if you use different model providers for trusted and untrusted?

### Context and Use Case

I would like to run evaluations faster on my laptop computer.

### Documentation Consulted

README
API Documentation

### Documentation Improvement Suggestion

Please add a "Best Practices for Running Experiments Quickly" section to the docs.
